I'll find merged pull requests that close Linear issues, then check the state of those Linear issues to ensure they're not 'done'. Let me start by retrieving the data.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Get all merged pull requests that close Linear issues
(def merged-prs (github_pull_requests {:state "merged"}))
(def prs-with-linear (filter :closes_linear merged-prs))

;; Get the Linear issue IDs from these PRs
(def linear-ids (map :closes_linear prs-with-linear))
linear-ids"}

text: Now I have the Linear issue IDs. I need to check which of these issues are not in 'done' state. Let me retrieve the Linear issues and filter them

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Get all Linear issues with the IDs from the PRs
(def linear-issues (linear_issues {:id linear-ids}))

;; Filter out issues that are 'done'
(def not-done-issues (remove #(= (:state %) "done") linear-issues))

;; Get the IDs of issues that are not done
(def not-done-ids (set (map :id not-done-issues)))
not-done-ids"}

text: Now I need to find which PRs close these Linear issues (the ones not in 'done' state) and compile the report:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Find PRs that close Linear issues not in 'done' state
(def matching-prs (filter #(contains? not-done-ids (:closes_linear %)) prs-with-linear))

;; Create the report with PR number and Linear issue id
(def report (map (fn [pr] {:pr (:id pr) :linear_issue (:closes_linear pr)}) matching-prs))

;; Get the total count
(def total-count (count matching-prs))

{:count total-count :prs…(+10)

text: I notice the PR numbers are showing as `null`. Let me check what the actual column name is for pull request identifiers in the github_pull_requests resource:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input: {"code":";; Let me check the structure of a pull request to see the column names\n(take 1 merged-prs)"}

data: {"value":[{"number":1000,"repo":"acme/api","title":"Onboarding funnel drop-off: add test for onboarding-funnel","author":"ivan","state":"merged","additions":571,"deletions":11,"created_at":"2026-05-13T00:00:00.000Z","merged_at":"2026-05-16T00:00:00.000Z","base":"main","body":"This change addresses o…(+30)

text: I see! The PR identifier is in the `:number` column, not `:id`. Let me fix my report:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Update report with correct PR number column
(def report-fixed (map (fn [pr] {:pr (:number pr) :linear_issue (:closes_linear pr)}) matching-prs))

;; Get total count
(def total-count-fixed (count matching-prs))

{:count total-count-fixed :prs report-fixed}"}

text: Perfect! Now I have the complete report. Let me also verify that there are indeed 13 merged PRs closing Linear issues, and all those Linear issues are not in 'done' state by checking if any are missing from my report:

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; Verify counts
(def all-prs-count (count prs-with-linear))
(def not-done-count (count matching-prs))
(def done-count (- all-prs-count not-done-count))

{:all_merged_prs_closing_linear all-prs-count
 :not_done_linear_issues not-done-count
 :done_linear_issues done-count}"}
